channel.title: HPKE on Cryspen
channel.description: Recent content in HPKE on Cryspen

item.title: An Executable HPKE Specification
item.description:
HPKE, published as RFC 9180, describes a scheme for hybrid public key encryption.

📚 Read our TL;DR on HPKE if you need more background on HPKE.

In this post, I describe the first executable HPKE specification using hacspec. It is not only an executable specification of HPKE, it is also an annotated version of the RFC that can be read instead of (or in addition to) the RFC. While the TL;DR on HPKE was intended for consumers or potential users of HPKE, this blog post is aimed at implementators that want to implement HPKE or understand it better.

item.title: TL;DR - Hybrid Public Key Encryption
item.description:
HPKE, defined in RFC 9180, is a CFRG standard that describes a scheme for hybrid public key encryption. It is co-authored by my Cryspen co-founder Karthikeyan Bhargavan and one of his PhD students Benjamin Lipp as part of his research at Inria.

This blog post will give a brief overview of the specification and describes some use cases.

If you want to learn more about the security proofs behind HPKE and the RFC process, Benjamin wrote an excellent blog post about it.
